title: Howmet Aerospace (HWM) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Howmet Aerospace (HWM).

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A 12.0% growth rate is projected. This is driven by robust 14.6% revenue growth, expanding profit margins near 18%, and the ongoing recovery in commercial aerospace build rates combined with increased defense spending.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: An 8.5% discount rate reflects Howmet's solid market position and entrenched customer relationships, balanced against the cyclical nature of the aerospace industry and its reliance on major OEMs.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 3.0% terminal growth rate assumes long-term stability in the aerospace sector, aligning roughly with historical global GDP growth as commercial air travel and defense needs normalize.

thesisParagraphs: Howmet Aerospace is uniquely positioned to capitalize on the multi-year upcycle in commercial aviation and the structural increase in global defense budgets. As airlines rush to modernize their fleets with more fuel-efficient aircraft, the demand for Howmet's specialized engine components—critical for higher operating temperatures and fuel efficiency—is soaring.

The company's immense switching costs and formidable intellectual property create an economic moat that allows it to capture a significant portion of the value created in the aerospace supply chain. While currently trading at a premium reflecting this strong position and anticipated growth, Howmet's ability to consistently expand margins and generate robust free cash flow makes it a compelling long-term compounder, albeit arguably overvalued at current prices.
verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: Why a 12% FCF growth rate for Howmet?
faq.answer: The 12% rate reflects strong recent revenue growth (14.6%) and an expanding margin profile, fueled by accelerating aircraft production rates and strong defense spending.

faq.question: What is Howmet's primary competitive advantage?
faq.answer: Its massive switching costs. Replacing a certified engine component supplier like Howmet requires years of testing and regulatory approval, creating an exceptionally sticky customer base.

faq.question: Why is the stock considered overvalued in this model?
faq.answer: While the fundamental business is exceptionally strong, the current price ($232.94) aggressively prices in future growth and margin expansion. Our DCF model yields an intrinsic value of $185.50, suggesting a negative margin of safety.
